summary: The config flag row manager path now bridges upward through a direct-call chain from the row helper to a spatial/grid-style root function.
0x140306406 currently has no direct callers found by rel32 callsite search, so it is the present owner-boundary for this path.
0x140306406 and 0x140306ac0 operate over client fields around 0x13c74, 0x13c78, 0x13c94, 0x13c95, 0x13c98, 0x13ca0, 0x13cc0, 0x13ce8, 0x13cec, 0x13cf0, and 0x13cf4.

The root side uses scalar floating-point conversion, bounds/state bytes, and pointer-span iteration, which is bounded evidence for a viewport/world-object spatial update path rather than bootstrap scheduling itself.
This bridge remains separate from the older startup/config side-effect path 0x1400252a0 -> 0x140024640 and 0x140024c40 -> 0x140023e60 -> raw block 0x1403da540.

The Reverser instruction decoder now covers the scalar/vector SIMD and three-operand IMUL forms needed by this bridge; regenerated root and bridge instruction artifacts have zero unknown instructions.

call_chain.role: current root boundary; spatial/grid root caller
immediate owner of the config flag row manager

key_operations: converts scalar XMM values to integer positions with CVTTSS2SI
scales scalar positions with MULSS constants at 0x140babd08 and 0x140babd24
stores converted position fields at 0x13cf0 and 0x13cf4
calls 0x140306ac0 and stores returned object/update pointers
root_boundary.inference: The function looks like a spatial or viewport object-update root. No direct caller was found, so the next owner is likely reached through an indirect dispatch, callback, or table-driven entry rather than a plain rel32 call.

tooling_progress.result: Decoded CVTTSS2SI, CVTTSD2SI, MULSS, MULSD, MOVSS, and MOVSD in PE instruction windows.

tooling_progress.capability: SIMD integer conversion and scalar compare/add forms
tooling_progress.result: Decoded MOVD, MOVQ, CVTDQ2PS, UCOMISS, COMISS, ADDSS, ADDSD, and ADDPS patterns seen in the bridge windows.
Decoded opcode 0x69 and 0x6b forms such as IMUL R12, RCX, 0x460 without desynchronizing later instructions.

next_targets: Trace indirect owner candidates for 0x140306406 because no plain direct caller was found.
Search vtable, descriptor, or callback tables for 0x140306406 and nearby entry thunks.
Map helper targets 0x1407f00f8, 0x140305ec0, and 0x140028e30 to name the root-boundary side effects.
Follow scalar constants 0x140babd08 and 0x140babd24 to label the spatial scale units.
Map sibling bridge targets around 0x1403a91b0, including 0x1403a8a40, 0x1403a95b0, 0x1403b77b0, and 0x1403ba480.
Keep the startup/config side-effect path separate while continuing JS5 and bootstrap mapping from earlier scheduler-readiness artifacts.
